content:
Why Markets Looks Like the Perfect Solution
The logic is clean: one store, one admin panel, automatic geolocation, local currencies, translations via Translate & Adapt. No duplicated infrastructure, no separate Shopify billing.
For a brand testing a new market on a limited budget, this is genuinely the smart starting point. Markets solves 80% of multi-regional challenges with minimal overhead.
But there’s a 20% that can quietly break your conversion — and almost nobody talks about it until the store is already live.

Where Markets Falls Short Technically
1. URL structure you don’t fully control
Markets defaults to subfolders: yourstore.com/en-eu/, yourstore.com/en-ca/. For most brands, fine. But if you're entering a market where a local domain is critical for trust — Germany, France, specific B2B segments — a .de or .fr domain converts differently than a subfolder on a US-based domain.
This isn’t an SEO debate. It’s behavioral: a shopper in the DACH region sees yourstore.com/en-de/ and reads "this isn't a local store." Especially in high-AOV categories where trust is part of the purchase decision.
2. Checkout is localized — but not completely
Shopify Markets gives you local currency and language on the front end. But checkout still has constraints: local payment methods like SEPA, iDEAL, and Bancontact connect through Shopify Payments — and in some regions, that integration isn’t seamless. If your target market has a strong attachment to a specific payment method and it doesn’t work without friction, you’re losing conversions at exactly the wrong moment.
3. Content strategy that doesn’t scale through a single store
Markets lets you translate content, but it doesn’t let you run fundamentally different product logic across markets without custom development. If your EU and US assortments differ significantly — different SKUs, shipping terms, promotional mechanics — managing that through one Shopify store becomes increasingly painful as the catalog grows.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

If you’re planning international expansion or you’ve already launched on Markets and you’re seeing conversion drop — at Devpie Digital, we audit exactly these kinds of setups: current architecture review and a recommendation built around your business model, not a template.
